title: 控方证人
summary:
During a legal consultation, Leonard Wall maintains his innocence regarding the death of Miss Emily French, despite facing a serious financial crisis. Wall explains that he first met Miss French on Oxford Street after helping her recover dropped packages. He later encountered her at a party hosted by his friend, George Harvey. Wall claims he only learned of Miss French's wealth and her eccentric lifestyle—including her residence in Crikwood and her ownership of eight cats—through Harvey.

Wall admits to visiting Miss French frequently, asserting that his motivation was not financial but a psychological response to her affection and his own indecisive nature. He states that he eventually began managing her business affairs because she claimed to be inexperienced with investments. His lawyer, Mr. Mehn, challenges this claim by noting that Miss French’s maid, Janet McKenzie, and her bank manager described her as a capable businesswoman. Mehn privately considers the possibility that Miss French may have feigned incompetence to flatter Wall.

When Mehn asks if Wall ever embezzled funds to alleviate his financial troubles, Wall emphatically denies any underhanded dealings. Wall argues that his strongest defense is a lack of motive, asserting that if he had courted her for money, her death would have thwarted his goals. However, Mehn reveals that Miss French left a will naming Wall as her primary beneficiary.
